title: Trim defaultSyncResources to app-token-viable resources
rationale: This CLI authenticates with a Twitch app access token (client_credentials, the default grant). The generator's defaultSyncResources listed 21 resources, but most require a user OAuth token (bits, streams-markers, twitch-helix-analytics*) or a required broadcaster_id/user_id/game_id/id parameter (games, videos, users, teams, clips, chat, extensions, entitlements). Out of the box those produce access-policy warnings or errors, so the default `sync` was mostly noise. Empirically verified the resources that succeed with an app token and no required param: games-top, streams, content-classification-labels, chat-emotes-global, eventsub-subscriptions. Trimmed the defaults to exactly those. knownSyncResourceNames() is left unchanged so every resource remains reachable via --resources for users who supply a user token or required params. Mirrors the library precedent of removing a credential-gated resource from the default set (oura webhook).

changes.summary: defaultSyncResources() now returns only games-top, streams, content-classification-labels, chat-emotes-global, eventsub-subscriptions (the app-token-viable, no-required-param set). knownSyncResourceNames() unchanged.
changes.validated_outcome: go build/vet pass; `sync` with no --resources succeeds with 0 warnings; each trimmed-in resource verified to return 200 with an app token (games-top 20, streams 20, content-classification-labels 7, chat-emotes-global 302, eventsub-subscriptions 0).
